items.text: Hi Maya, Quick update — the Photography Club meeting has moved to this Thursday at 6:30 PM in Room 118, Bell Hall, instead of our usual Tuesday slot. Please bring your $5 supplies fee if you haven't paid it yet. See you there! Best, Dev

questions.stem: According to the email, when will the Photography Club meeting now take place?
questions.explanation: The email states the meeting has moved to Thursday at 6:30 PM, replacing the usual Tuesday slot, so option A is correct.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $5 supplies fee?

options.A: It covers snacks for every meeting.
options.B: It must be paid at the door.
options.C: It will be refunded to members later.
options.D: It was already paid by some students.

questions.explanation: Dev asks Maya to bring the fee only 'if you haven't paid it yet,' which implies some members have already paid, making D the best inference.

items.text: NOTICE: Fire alarm testing will be conducted in Whitman Hall on Monday, October 14, from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM. Alarms will sound intermittently during this period. Residents are advised to remain in their rooms and may contact the Facilities Office with any questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the fire alarm testing be conducted?
questions.explanation: The notice specifies that testing will be conducted in Whitman Hall, so option C is correct.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the alarms during the testing period?

options.A: The alarms signal a test, not danger.
options.B: The alarms will ring without stopping.
options.C: The alarms require an immediate evacuation.
options.D: The alarms are newly installed this week.

questions.explanation: Because residents are told to remain in their rooms while alarms sound intermittently, the alarms clearly signal a scheduled test rather than a real emergency, making A the best inference.

items.text: Dear Sam, To replace your lost student ID, please visit the Campus Card Office in Room 210, Foster Center, before Friday, March 6. Bring a photo and the $10 replacement fee. Your new card will be ready within two business days. Thanks, Registrar Staff

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Sam bring to the Campus Card Office?

options.B: An ID card and a receipt

questions.explanation: The email instructs Sam to bring a photo and the $10 replacement fee, which option D paraphrases as a photograph and ten dollars.

questions.stem: According to the email, when will Sam's new card be ready?
questions.explanation: The email states the new card will be ready within two business days, which option B restates as two working days.

items.text: NOTICE: Fire alarm testing is scheduled in Bradley Hall on Wednesday, November 5, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM. All alarms will be activated briefly. Tampering with any alarm during testing will result in a $50 fine. Questions may be directed to the Housing Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the fire alarm testing occur?
questions.explanation: The notice schedules testing from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM, which option C restates as between 2:00 and 4:00 PM.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the alarms during the testing?

options.A: The alarms will stay silent throughout.
options.B: The alarms do not mean real danger.
options.C: The alarms require an immediate evacuation.
options.D: The alarms will ring for the entire day.

questions.explanation: Since the alarms are activated only briefly as part of scheduled testing, they do not indicate a genuine fire, making B the best inference.
